claim: Currier A and Currier B sub-corpora have glyph unigram distributions that are more statistically divergent from each other than any random equal-sized split of the same combined corpus, confirming they encode under structurally distinct cipher tables rather than merely reflecting scribal style variation.
evidence_for: Multiple high-confidence beliefs (0.96, 0.958, 0.942) converge on A/B using distinct cipher alphabets
Currier A (11,022 words) vs B (23,766 words) size difference suggests different scribal contexts, not random variation
EVA 'q' at 98.9% word-initial frequency is the strongest positional constraintâ€”if A and B use different tables, q's positional profile should differ between sub-corpora
evidence_against: If A and B encode the same language with the same cipher but different register or topic, glyph distributions could diverge due to content rather than cipher table differences
Section overlap with A/B (herbal is predominantly A, biological predominantly B) confounds topic vs. cipher table as explanatory variable
test: Compute Jensen-Shannon divergence JSD(P_A, P_B) on 25-glyph unigram distributions. Bootstrap 10,000 random splits of the full 34,788-word corpus (excluding zodiac labels) into sizes matching A and B, compute JSD for each. Report empirical p-value of observed JSD versus bootstrap null.
test_metric: Jensen-Shannon divergence between Currier A and B glyph unigrams vs. bootstrap null distribution
test_threshold: Confirm if observed JSD exceeds the 99th percentile of the bootstrap null (empirical p < 0.01); deny if observed JSD falls below the 95th percentile
tests_remaining: Compute Jensen-Shannon divergence JSD(P_A, P_B) on 25-glyph unigram distributions. Bootstrap 10,000 random splits of the full 34,788-word corpus (excluding zodiac labels) into sizes matching A and B, compute JSD for each. Report empirical p-value of observed JSD versus bootstrap null.
reasoning: A statistically significant glyph distribution divergence beyond topic-driven variation would prove the cipher-table-distinction hypothesis and constrain decipherment to dual-alphabet models; a null result would collapse A/B hypotheses to scribal style
